what_it_does: This file is a channel attachment metadata record: it declares an image attachment with id att-7826712f in the design-source channel, points to the image asset image://design-source/capital-raise/p-18, and records the ISO8601 timestamp when it was added.
observations: The entire content is YAML frontmatter with no body text
target uses the URI scheme image://design-source/capital-raise/p-18
standouts: The target URI uses an image:// scheme with a p-18 identifier
It is a typed image attachment in the design-source channel
It records a precise addition timestamp
conventions: Id prefix att- for attachment records
References an asset by a channel-scoped URI
Minimal record with only required fields
concerns: No caption, alt text, or description of the image content
No asset hash, MIME type, dimensions, or version
The meaning of p-18 is not explained in the file
notes: A bare attachment ledger entry that links the design-source channel to image p-18 in the capital-raise folder.
questions: What is the actual content of the referenced image?
Is the image:// URI resolvable and versioned?
purpose_vs_actual: The file has no explicit prose purpose, but its fields exactly match an attachment record: it identifies, types, targets, and timestamps an attached asset.
intention_signals: It appears intended to catalog an image asset as part of the design-source channel's capital-raise materials, preserving a precise time of attachment for traceability.
novelty: The image:// URI scheme and the p-18 identifier are the only non-generic elements; otherwise it is a conventional metadata stub.
contribution: It contributes a typed, timestamped binding between a channel and a specific image asset, which a fused system would keep as a provenance and retrieval anchor.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter attachment record in the design-source channel referencing image asset capital-raise/p-18 via an image:// URI and an ISO8601 addition timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To log that an image asset was attached to the design-source channel at a specific time.
